steps.title: Spot the contradiction in the evidence
steps.content: The recent finding is that economists' estimates vary by more than a factor of five even within the same country and timeframe. Since country-specific characteristics like oil import-export ratios are constant within a country, they cannot explain this within-country variation—the source must be something else.

steps.title: Match to the correct explanation
steps.content: C identifies methodological discrepancies as the source of heterogeneity in economists' estimates. The others revert to country-specific explanations (D), misidentify measurement sensitivity as the issue (B), or invoke an unrelated mechanism (A). Since country-specific characteristics are constant within a single country, methodological differences in how studies are conducted offer the only logical explanation for the variation in estimates. That matches choice C — C is correct.
